title: Research
text: Computational geospatial science validated through field research

title: Research
text:
Overview
Our research agenda integrates deep learning, machine learning, and geospatial cloud computing with systematic field-based ground validation. Every project follows our core philosophy: computational analysis must be validated through rigorous field survey and exploratory research to produce meaningful, reliable geographic outcomes.

title: Research
text:
Current Projects

1. LULC Classification Using Deep Learning with Field Validation


Status: ● Active | Duration: 2024 – Present
Developing and comparing deep learning architectures — CNN, U-Net, ResNet-50, and Vision Transformers — for automated land use/land cover (LULC) classification from Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery. All classification results are validated through systematic ground-truth field surveys using GPS-based stratified random sampling across diverse landscape units.
Methods & Tools:

TensorFlow/PyTorch for model development in Python
Google Earth Engine for image preprocessing and composite generation
QGIS for spatial data preparation, visualization, and map layout
Field surveys with GPS/GNSS for training and validation sample collection
Accuracy assessment: Overall Accuracy, Kappa Coefficient, F1-score, Confusion Matrix

Key Outcomes:

94.7% overall accuracy for 8-class LULC with field-validated CNN model
Comparative analysis of 5 DL architectures across 3 geographic regions
1,200+ field-validated ground-truth sample points collected






LULC Classification







2. Multi-Temporal Change Detection Using GEE & Machine Learning





Change Detection Analysis




Status: ● Active | Duration: 2023 – Present
Large-scale land cover change analysis using Google Earth Engine’s cloud computing platform for multi-temporal Landsat and Sentinel-2 time series processing. Machine learning classifiers (Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM) are applied in R and Python, with change trajectories validated through sequential field surveys and historical land records.
Methods & Tools:

Google Earth Engine JavaScript/Python API for annual composites and spectral indices
Random Forest and Gradient Boosting classifiers in R (caret, randomForest)
Python (scikit-learn) for model comparison and feature importance analysis
QGIS for post-classification filtering and cartographic outputs
Field-based sequential exploratory research at change hotspots

Key Outcomes:

30-year land cover change analysis spanning 1993–2023
Identification of urban expansion corridors with 92% change detection accuracy
Field verification at 200+ randomly selected change/no-change locations






3. Spatial Climate Variability Modeling with ML & Ground Observations


Status: ● Active | Duration: 2024 – Present
Machine learning-driven spatial analysis of climate variability patterns including temperature anomalies, precipitation trends, and urban heat islands. Models are trained using gridded reanalysis datasets (ERA5, CRU) and satellite-derived Land Surface Temperature (LST), validated against meteorological ground station observations and field-deployed weather sensors.
Methods & Tools:

Python (xarray, rioxarray) for climate data processing
Random Forest, XGBoost, and ensemble stacking in R and Python
Google Earth Engine for MODIS LST and NDVI time series extraction
QGIS for spatial interpolation (IDW, Kriging) and vulnerability mapping
In-situ temperature and precipitation data from field-deployed sensors

Key Outcomes:

Urban heat island intensity maps validated against 50+ field measurement points
Precipitation trend analysis covering 40-year period with 85% spatial R²
Climate vulnerability index maps for 3 metropolitan study areas






Climate Variability Analysis







4. Crop Type Mapping with SAR-Optical Fusion & Ground Survey
Status: ● Active | Duration: 2025 – Present
Fusioning Sentinel-1 SAR with Sentinel-2 optical data using attention-based deep learning architectures for crop type classification in cloud-prone regions. Training data is collected through extensive ground-truth field surveys involving GPS-referenced plot-level crop identification and farmer interviews.
Methodology Pipeline:

GEE-based multi-temporal SAR (VV, VH) and optical (10 spectral bands) composite generation
Attention-based CNN fusion model developed in PyTorch
Time-series phenology analysis using Dynamic Time Warping in Python
Field survey across 300+ agricultural plots for crop type and planting date validation
Accuracy assessment through stratified cross-validation and independent test set evaluation




5. Urban Flood Vulnerability Assessment: GIS-ML Integration
Status: ● In Progress | Duration: 2025 – Present
Developing a multi-criteria GIS framework integrated with machine learning for urban flood vulnerability mapping. The project combines QGIS-based terrain analysis (slope, TWI, flow accumulation), socioeconomic indicators, and land cover data, with Random Forest and Logistic Regression models. Results are validated through field-based flood occurrence surveys and community interviews.
Tools Stack:

QGIS: DEM processing, terrain derivatives, spatial overlay analysis
Python (scikit-learn): ML model development and hyperparameter tuning
R (sf, tmap): Spatial visualization, statistical analysis, and map production
GPS-based field surveys at historically flood-affected locations




6. GEE-Based Vegetation Health Monitoring with Ground Validation
Status: ● Active | Duration: 2024 – Present
Automated vegetation health assessment using NDVI, EVI, and SAVI indices computed in Google Earth Engine from Sentinel-2 and MODIS data. Trend analysis using Mann-Kendall tests and Sen’s slope estimation in R. Anomaly detection validated through field-measured LAI (Leaf Area Index) and biomass sampling.

Spatial Analysis MeetsIntelligent Computing


We integrate deep learning, machine learning, and geospatial technologies — including Google Earth Engine, QGIS, Python, and R — with rigorous field-based survey research. Our work bridges computational geography and ground-truth validation to generate actionable, evidence-based insights for real-world geographic challenges.

title: About Us
text: Where spatial computing meets field-based geographic inquiry

title: About Us
text:
Our Mission
The Spatial Geography Lab is committed to advancing geographic science through the integration of cutting-edge computational methods — deep learning, machine learning, and cloud-based geospatial platforms — with rigorous field-based survey research. We believe that the most meaningful geographic insights emerge when satellite-derived analyses are validated through systematic ground-truth observations, creating a complete research pipeline from data acquisition to actionable outcomes.

 Vision Statement
To be a leading center of excellence in integrated spatial geographic research — combining the analytical power of deep learning, GIS, and remote sensing with the authenticity of field-based ground validation — empowering communities and decision-makers with reliable, evidence-based geographic intelligence.

title: About Us
text:
Our Approach
What distinguishes our lab is our commitment to integrated research methodology:

Computational Analysis First — We leverage deep learning (CNNs, vision transformers), machine learning (Random Forest, XGBoost, SVM), and cloud platforms (Google Earth Engine) for large-scale spatial analysis.
Ground Validation Always — Every computational result is subjected to rigorous field validation through sequential exploratory research, GPS-based ground-truth campaigns, and systematic in-situ observations.
Open & Reproducible — We develop our workflows in Python and R, share our code openly, and use open-source GIS tools (QGIS, GRASS GIS) to ensure reproducibility across the research community.

title: About Us
text:
Core Values






Scientific Rigor
We uphold the highest standards of geographic research — every remote sensing product is accuracy-assessed, every model is validated, every conclusion is field-verified.







Open Science
We share our datasets, code, and methodologies openly. Our Python and R scripts, GEE workflows, and QGIS models are freely available on GitHub for the community.







Collaboration
We believe that impactful geographic research emerges from interdisciplinary teamwork — connecting geographers, computer scientists, ecologists, and planners.

title: About Us
text:
Research Focus Areas
Our research operates across six interconnected domains:

Deep Learning for Remote Sensing — Developing CNN, U-Net, ResNet, and transformer architectures for automated land cover classification, semantic segmentation, and object detection from satellite imagery.
Machine Learning & Spatial Prediction — Applying Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, SVM, and ensemble methods for spatial modeling, habitat suitability, vulnerability assessment, and change prediction in Python and R.
Google Earth Engine Analytics — Cloud-based planetary-scale analysis for time-series change detection, composite generation, spectral index computation, and large-area land monitoring workflows.
GIS & QGIS-based Spatial Analysis — Advanced spatial analysis using QGIS, including multi-criteria decision analysis (MCDA), network analysis, terrain derivatives, suitability modeling, and cartographic visualization.
Field Survey & Ground Validation — Systematic field campaigns using GPS/GNSS equipment, stratified sampling designs, sequential exploratory research methods, and accuracy assessment protocols (confusion matrix, Kappa, F1-score).
Python & R Geospatial Programming — Building reproducible spatial data science pipelines using GeoPandas, Rasterio, TensorFlow/PyTorch (Python) and sf, terra, caret/tidymodels (R) for spatial analysis and modeling.

title: About Us
text:
Lab History & Milestones






2015

Lab Founded
Established with an initial focus on GIS-based spatial analysis and remote sensing applications using QGIS and ERDAS IMAGINE.








2017

Python & R Integration
Adopted Python and R as primary research languages, integrating GeoPandas, Rasterio, sf, and terra into all research workflows.








2019

Google Earth Engine Adoption
Began leveraging Google Earth Engine for large-scale, cloud-based remote sensing analysis, enabling continental-scale land monitoring.








2021

Deep Learning Research Program
Launched dedicated deep learning research, applying CNNs and transformers to satellite image classification and achieving state-of-the-art accuracy.








2023

Field Validation Protocols
Formalized our field survey and ground-truth validation methodology, establishing standard protocols for sequential exploratory research and accuracy assessment.








2025

Open Geospatial Platform
Launched an open-access platform sharing our datasets, trained models, GEE scripts, and QGIS workflows with the global research community.

title: About Us
text:
Infrastructure & Facilities







Facility
Description




GPU Computing Cluster
NVIDIA A100/RTX workstations for training deep learning models (TensorFlow, PyTorch) on large satellite image datasets


GIS Workstations
Dedicated workstations equipped with QGIS, ArcGIS Pro, SNAP/ESA, and Google Earth Engine access


Field Survey Equipment
GNSS/RTK receivers, handheld GPS units, portable spectrometers, and field tablets for ground-truth data collection


UAV/Drone Lab
DJI Phantom 4 RTK and Matrice 300 RTK for aerial survey, photogrammetry, and site documentation


Geospatial Data Library
Archived satellite imagery (Landsat, Sentinel-1/2, MODIS), DEM data, and field-collected training/validation datasets


Collaborative Workspace
Seminar room with projection displays for group coding sessions, journal clubs, and research presentations





 Want to Learn More?
We welcome inquiries from prospective students, researchers, and collaborators. Contact us to discuss potential opportunities and partnerships.

title: Gallery
text: Visualizing our spatial research — from satellite to field

title: Gallery
text:
Research Visualizations
Our research produces rich geospatial outputs spanning deep learning-based classification maps, GEE time-series analyses, terrain models, and field survey documentation.


 ::: {.gallery-overlay} #### LULC Classification Map Deep learning (CNN)-based automated land use/land cover classification from Sentinel-2 imagery, validated with field-collected GPS points.

title: Gallery
text:
Fieldwork & Ground Validation






GPS Ground-Truth Surveys
Systematic GPS-based field campaigns collecting training and validation samples at 1,200+ stratified random locations for remote sensing accuracy assessment.







Sequential Exploratory Research
Field-based exploratory investigation of landscape patterns, verifying satellite-derived change hotspots, and documenting ground conditions through structured observation protocols.







UAV/Drone Surveys
Drone-based aerial surveys using RTK-enabled platforms for photogrammetric mapping, crop monitoring, and centimeter-resolution site documentation.

title: Resources
text: Tools and knowledge for the geospatial community

title: Resources
text:
Overview
We are committed to open science and reproducible research. Below you’ll find curated resources including our open-source tools, Python/R code, GEE scripts, QGIS models, field survey protocols, and recommended learning materials.

title: Resources
text:
Open-Source Tools & Code






LULC-DL Toolkit
Python library for deep learning-based land cover classification from satellite imagery, with built-in accuracy assessment and field validation utilities.

 GitHub Repository
 Documentation
 pip install lulc-dl








GEE Workflow Library
Collection of Google Earth Engine JavaScript and Python scripts for change detection, composite generation, spectral indices, and time-series analysis.

 GitHub Repository
 Script Catalog
 GEE Code Editor








R-Spatial Analysis Pack
R package with functions for spatial prediction, geostatistical analysis, accuracy assessment, and automated report generation for geographic research.

 GitHub Repository
 Documentation

title: Resources
text:
Recommended Software Stack

GIS & Mapping

QGIS — Primary open-source GIS for visualization, analysis, and cartographic production
GRASS GIS — Advanced raster and vector processing, terrain analysis
Google Earth Engine — Cloud platform for planetary-scale analysis



Programming Languages

Python — GeoPandas, Rasterio, scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, xarray
R — sf, terra, stars, tmap, caret, randomForest, rgee, tidyverse
JavaScript — GEE Code Editor, Leaflet, D3.js for web mapping



Remote Sensing

SNAP/ESA — Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 optical processing
ERDAS IMAGINE — Advanced image classification and photogrammetry



Field Survey

GPS Essentials / SW Maps — Android GPS data collection apps
KoBoToolbox — Open-source field data collection platform
ODK (Open Data Kit) — Mobile data collection for ground-truth surveys

title: Resources
text:
Useful External Resources

Data Sources

USGS Earth Explorer — Landsat, Sentinel, and other satellite data
Copernicus Open Access Hub — Free Sentinel-1, -2, -3 data
NASA Earthdata — NASA earth science data archive
OpenStreetMap — Open collaborative mapping data
Natural Earth — Free vector and raster map data



Learning Platforms

Geocomputation with R — Open textbook for spatial data science in R
Automating GIS Processes — University of Helsinki Python GIS course
EO-College — Free courses on Earth observation
GEE Developers Guide — Official GEE documentation



 Suggest a Resource
Know of a great resource we should include? Let us know and we’ll add it to this page!
